# Transcrição: TBT Week 6 Teleclass

- **Fonte:** TBT Week 6 Teleclass
- **Duração:** 1:06:13
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

And I just wanted to tell everyone today we're going to be going through the presentation rather quickly so that we can answer more of your questions. So just seeing that in mind, raise your hand right away so we know how many questions there are and we can get to you right away. Great. So, star two, if you're on the phone, press star two to raise your hand. All right. So welcome everyone to Total Breakthrough Training. This is Master Unit 2 and Modules 1, 2, and 3 will begin talking about the Stagner family as the sequence of interventions. And so let's talk about, we're going to talk about some of the ways that Tony perceived the Ron and Reese Stagner situation and how he addressed it. So, Ron and Reese Stagner, go ahead. You know, Mark, since I have a delay on my slides, maybe I should go first so that while I'm talking, you get your slide ready. That sounds great. That sounds great. So one of the, we're first going to cover some of the bigger themes of working with this couple. So the surface problem was financial loss. We all heard their story. Problem was one of the couple being divided and very critical of each other. So we heard Marie say that their relationship was like roommates. We heard Ron call the things she did, tiddly things. And Tony really picked up right away on the fact that the first step for these two people was to pull them together and unite them as a couple. Yeah. And so that's, you know, when you're a coach and you're looking at a situation, you kind of have to decide what is the most urgent, what is the kind of showstopper problem that's going on. And in this case, you could, you know, Ron, you keep working out the business, but if they're about to break up and they can't even operate together functionally, then he needs to strengthen the team first. And this, in fact, was a theme with most of the interventions and breakthrough was that you had to help the marriage become stronger together. So he took them through a series of challenges. And so the first challenge was skydiving, right? And so the principle of the team was... Skydiving. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry.
